name: Jeremiah
shortDescription: Jeremiah is a major prophet in the Hebrew Bible, known for his prophecies of destruction and eventual restoration of Judah.
description: Jeremiah is a central figure in Jewish prophecy, serving as a major prophet in the Hebrew Bible. His prophetic ministry spanned a tumultuous period in Judah's history, from the reign of King Josiah to the Babylonian exile. Jeremiah's prophecies are characterized by stark warnings of impending doom due to Judah's idolatry and social injustice. He foretold the destruction of Jerusalem and the Temple, events that came to pass in 586 BCE. However, Jeremiah's message also included a promise of restoration and a new covenant with God, offering hope for the future. His prophecies are recorded in the Book of Jeremiah, a complex and multifaceted text that reflects the prophet's personal struggles, his interactions with the people, and his profound spiritual insights. The image of the potter and the clay (referenced in jewish_potter-and-clay) is strongly associated with Jeremiah's teachings, symbolizing God's sovereignty and Judah's malleability. Jeremiah’s struggles and messages resonate throughout Jewish theological and philosophical thought.

sources.title: The Jewish Study Bible
sources.author: Berlin, Adele and Brettler, Marc Zvi

sources.title: Reading the Old Testament: An Introduction
sources.author: Barry L. Bandstra
